terms.def: Latest Estimate. The rolling re-forecast that blends YTD actuals with forward projections for the balance of the year. Feb 2026 LE is the current base version.
Annual Operating Plan. The approved budget set at year start, signed off by the Board. Used as the primary variance benchmark throughout the year.
Prior Year. Full-year or YTD actuals from the equivalent period one year earlier. Used as a secondary comparison alongside AOP.
Year to Date. Cumulative actuals from January through the latest closed month.
Favourable / Unfavourable. Convention: revenue above budget is FAV; cost above budget is UNF. Displayed in green / red respectively.
Difference between Actual and plan (AOP or LE). Computed as Actual minus Budget for revenue lines; Budget minus Actual for cost lines — so a positive number always means favourable.
A month locked in the database after actuals have been signed off. No further edits are permitted without admin unlock. Displayed with a lock icon in the Data Sources calendar.
Total billed sales to customers (kWh × tariff rate). Excludes fuel-cost pass-through surcharges which are separately tracked under FCAM.
Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation & Amortisation. Core operational profitability; the primary covenant and management performance metric.
Earnings Before Interest & Tax. EBITDA less total depreciation and impairment charges.
Net Profit Before Tax. EBIT less net financing costs (interest expense + FX loss − interest income + FX gain).
Net Profit After Tax. Bottom-line earnings after income tax expense.
Operations & Maintenance. The main controllable cost category, split into approximately 15 sub-lines (Labour, Contractors, Materials, Insurance, etc.).
Variable O&M. Fuel-linked operating cost per kWh generated by JPS-owned thermal plant.
Fixed O&M. Standing charges unrelated to generation volume, such as maintenance contracts and fixed labour.
Foreign Exchange Gain / Loss. Calculated as (Actual Rate − Budget Rate) × Net USD-denominated monetary position. A weaker Jamaican dollar vs budget produces an FX Loss.
Fuel Cost Adjustment Mechanism. OUR-approved tariff surcharge that passes fuel cost movements directly to customers, reducing net fuel exposure on the P&L.
Right-of-Use Asset. The capitalised present value of future lease payments under IFRS 16. Recognised for IPP PPAs, land, vehicles, and property leases.
Capital Work in Progress. CapEx spend on projects not yet commissioned and transferred to Fixed Assets. Once complete, transferred to the FA Register.
Property, Plant & Equipment. The net book value of JPS-owned fixed assets after accumulated depreciation.
Days Sales Outstanding. (Closing Trade Receivables ÷ Monthly Billing) × 30. Measures how quickly customers pay. JPS target: under 45 days.
Days Payable Outstanding. (Closing Trade Payables ÷ Monthly COGS) × 30. Measures how quickly JPS pays its suppliers.
Total interest-bearing borrowings less unrestricted cash. Key leverage metric monitored by lenders and the Board.
Leverage ratio. Lender covenant ceiling is typically 4.0×. JPS internal target: 3.5× or below.
Debt Service Coverage Ratio. EBITDA ÷ Total Debt Service (principal + interest due in the period). Covenant minimum: 1.20×.
Operating Cash Flow. Cash generated from the core business before investing and financing activities.
Free Cash Flow. OCF less CapEx. Indicates cash available for debt service and dividends after maintaining the asset base.
Capital Expenditure. Spend on new or upgraded fixed assets. Split into Sustaining CapEx (maintenance of existing assets) and Growth CapEx (expansion).
Change in (Receivables + Inventory − Payables). A positive movement equals a cash inflow — e.g. collecting receivables faster than the prior period.
Kilowatt-hour / Gigawatt-hour / Megawatt-hour. Units of electrical energy. 1 GWh = 1,000 MWh = 1,000,000 kWh.
Megawatt. Unit of electrical power (capacity). JPS total installed capacity is approximately 618 MW.
Independent Power Producer. Third-party generators that sell power to JPS under a PPA. Current IPPs: SJPC, JEP, JPPC, WKPP, Wigton.
Power Purchase Agreement. Long-term contract between JPS and an IPP defining energy purchase volume, capacity charge, and fuel cost pass-through terms.
South Jamaica Power Company. JPS's largest IPP; 190 MW LNG-fired combined-cycle plant at Old Harbour Bay. Accounted for under IFRS 16.
Jamaica Energy Partners. IPP operating a 74 MW HFO plant at Old Harbour Bay.
Jamaica Private Power Company. IPP at Bogue, St. James.
West Kingston Power Partners. Diesel / HFO peaking plant.
Wigton Windfarm. 62.7 MW wind generation facility; government-owned, sells to JPS under a PPA.
Heavy Fuel Oil. Primary thermal generation fuel for older JPS and IPP plant. Higher emissions, lower cost per kWh than ADO.
Liquefied Natural Gas. Cleaner-burning generation fuel used by SJPC. Sourced via New Fortress Energy (NFE).
Automotive Diesel Oil. Distillate fuel used for peaking and backup plant. The most expensive fuel type per kWh generated.
Fuel efficiency measure expressed as BTU per kWh (or kJ/kWh). Lower value means more efficient. Used to convert fuel price ($/MMBTU) into generation cost ($/kWh).
Transmission & Distribution Losses. Energy lost between generation and customer billing, including technical losses and non-technical losses (theft). Expressed as a percentage of GWh generated.
Total electricity demand on the JPS grid at a given point in time (MW). Peak system load drives capacity planning and dispatch scheduling.
The merit-order process of committing and scheduling generation units to meet demand at least cost.
Office of Utilities Regulation. The independent regulator that sets JPS tariff rates, reviews performance against licence targets, and approves fuel surcharges.
Performance-Based Rate-making. The OUR tariff framework that links JPS's allowed revenue to reliability and efficiency targets. Current licence runs to 2027.
Capital Recovery Factor. The annualised cost-of-capital factor used in tariff calculations to recover regulated asset investment over its useful life.
Residential Tariff class. Low-voltage domestic customers (the largest customer count). OUR-regulated rates with subsidised tiers for low-usage households.
General Service — small commercial & industrial. Low-voltage non-residential customers.
Large Power — Medium Voltage. Industrial and commercial customers on 12 kV or 24 kV supply.
Large Power — High Voltage. Major industrial customers on 69 kV transmission-level supply.
Street Lighting. OUR-regulated tariff for public lighting supplied to municipalities and NROCC.
Distributed Generation / Renewable buyback. Net-billing rate for customer-owned solar installations feeding excess generation to the grid.
Cost-Benefit Analysis. Required by OUR for major capital investments to demonstrate net benefit to consumers before approval.

Lease accounting standard effective 2019. Replaces straight-line operating lease expense with: (a) ROU asset depreciation on the P&L and (b) lease liability interest. JPS applies this to all IPP PPAs and significant property and vehicle leases.

Impairment of Assets. Requires testing assets for impairment whenever indicators exist. Impairment charges are always shown as a distinct line in this platform — never combined with regular depreciation.
Employee Benefits. Governs the accounting for JPS's defined-benefit pension obligation. Actuarial remeasurements go through OCI (Other Comprehensive Income), not through the P&L.
Financial Instruments. Governs classification, measurement, and expected credit loss (ECL) provisioning on trade receivables.
Other Comprehensive Income. Items recognised in equity but not on the face of the P&L — e.g. pension remeasurements and cash flow hedge reserves.
Fixed Asset Register. The source-of-truth schedule of all capitalised assets, showing cost, accumulated depreciation, and net book value by asset class.
A condensed month-end summary (Revenue, EBITDA, NPAT vs budget) distributed to senior management within 3 business days of month close.
Core platform database table. Stores all financial values keyed by (version_id, line_id, period_id). Every P&L, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow number flows through this table.
A named snapshot of the financial model — e.g. '2026 AOP' or 'Feb 2026 LE'. Multiple versions can coexist; the platform displays the active version selected in settings.
Admin action that freezes a closed month in the database, preventing further data changes. Represented by a lock icon in the Data Sources calendar.
